steps.title: Find f(2) using the functional equation
steps.content:
We are given that $f(cx) = x - 8$ for all values of $x$. To find $f(2)$, we need to determine what value of $x$ makes $cx = 2$. Solving for $x$: $x = \frac{2}{c}$. Now substitute this into the functional equation:
$$f(2) = f(cx) = x - 8 = \frac{2}{c} - 8$$

steps.title: Solve for c using f(2) = 35
steps.content:
We are told that $f(2) = 35$. From the previous step, we have $f(2) = \frac{2}{c} - 8$. Setting these equal:
$$\frac{2}{c} - 8 = 35$$
Add 8 to both sides:
$$\frac{2}{c} = 43$$
Multiply both sides by $c$ and divide by 43:
$$c = \frac{2}{43} \approx 0.0465$$
